title: verbascum × subvacillans
intro: Verbascum × subvacillans, a hybrid plant species of the mullein genus, is a biennial herb native to Europe and Asia, known for its large, showy flowers and velvety leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to its high mucilage content. The health benefits of Verbascum × subvacillans include relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to soothe skin irritations and wounds. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of the plant can be used to make teas, infusions, and ointments, which are often used to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
